name: Examples and Reference Flow
description: Deliver the three Flow Packages the spec names as first-class artifacts: `examples/hello-world` (smallest valid flow), `examples/hello-world-mocked` (proves the MockProvider path with no claude installed), and `@ganderbite/flow-codebase-discovery` (the canonical M3 reference flow with an HTML report). All three match the §7 Flow Package format end-to-end.

waves.name: examples/hello-world
waves.description: Build the smallest valid flow per §9.1 M1 acceptance #3. In `examples/hello-world/`: `package.json` (name: 'hello-world', private: true, deps: @relay/core workspace), `flow.ts` (defineFlow named 'hello-world' v0.1.0, two prompt steps: greet → summarize; input: z.object({ name: z.string() }); outputs an artifact `greeting.md`), `prompts/01_greet.md`, `prompts/02_summarize.md`, `tsconfig.json` extending base, `README.md` with the §7.4 sections. Add a build script so `pnpm -C examples/hello-world build` produces `dist/flow.js` consumable by the CLI. Must run against real `claude` CLI on a Max subscription with zero API charges.

waves.name: examples/hello-world-mocked
waves.description: Build the mocked twin per §9.1 M1 acceptance #4 — proves the Provider abstraction holds with no claude binary. In `examples/hello-world-mocked/`: identical flow shape to hello-world but the entry point `run-mocked.ts` constructs a ProviderRegistry containing a MockProvider (from @relay/core/testing) with canned responses keyed by step id, then calls `createRunner({ providers: registry, defaultProvider: 'mock' }).run(flow, input)`. Include `package.json`, `flow.ts`, prompts (same content as hello-world is fine), `run-mocked.ts`, `tsconfig.json`, `README.md` explaining how the mock substitutes. No Claude binary needed.

waves.name: codebase-discovery package scaffold
waves.description: Create the reference flow package at `packages/flows/codebase-discovery/` (add `packages/flows/*` to the pnpm workspace root in task_1's pnpm-workspace.yaml — update that file too). `package.json` matches §7.2 exactly: name '@ganderbite/flow-codebase-discovery', version '0.1.0', type module, main dist/flow.js, files array, peerDeps @relay/core ^1.0.0, relay metadata block (displayName, tags, estimatedCostUsd min 0.20 / max 0.80, estimatedDurationMin 5–20, audience ['pm','dev']). `tsconfig.json` extending base. Empty `schemas/` and `prompts/` dirs prepared for later tasks in this wave+next. `README.md` following §7.4's full section ordering. `examples/sample-output.html` placeholder.

waves.name: codebase-discovery flow.ts and schemas
waves.description: Write `packages/flows/codebase-discovery/flow.ts` matching Appendix A exactly: InventorySchema, EntitiesSchema in `schemas/`, imported into flow.ts. Four prompt steps (inventory → entities + services in parallel → report). Steps use the tool allowlist ['Read','Glob','Grep']. Input schema: z.object({ repoPath: z.string(), audience: z.enum(['pm','dev','both']).default('both') }). Report step output is `artifact: 'report.html'`. Use `contextFrom` to inject prior handoffs into downstream prompts.

waves.name: codebase-discovery HTML report template
waves.description: Create `packages/flows/codebase-discovery/templates/report.html.ejs` — a single-file HTML report containing the six sections per §9.3 acceptance (overview, inventory, entities, services, design review, recommendations). Use inline CSS (no external assets) so the artifact is self-contained. The report prompt (task_71) will load this template and ask Claude to fill it — or alternatively instruct Claude to render directly — document the choice inside the file. Also add an `examples/sample-output.html` populated with a small real sample rendered against the relay monorepo as a fixture.

waves.name: codebase-discovery prompts
waves.description: Write the four prompt files: `prompts/01_inventory.md` (instructs Claude to enumerate packages with path/name/language/entryPoints matching InventorySchema; must use Read/Glob/Grep; return JSON), `prompts/02_entities.md` (reads {{inventory}} context; identifies models/services/controllers/utils per package; returns JSON matching EntitiesSchema), `prompts/03_services.md` (reads {{inventory}} context; identifies cross-cutting services like auth, db, cache, queues; free-form JSON), `prompts/04_report.md` (reads inventory + entities + services contexts; renders the full HTML report by filling the template from task_70 with the collected data; output is the raw HTML string that becomes report.html). Tune each prompt for the PM audience path from the input schema default.
